name: BoundingBox
summary: Note: that the bounding box returned for face-me components is the center of its entire range of motion. This behavior changed in SketchUp 7.1. In 7.0 and earlier, the .bounds method would return the bounds around the face-me component's current, visible center. Bounding boxes are three-dimensional boxes (eight corners), aligned with the axes, that surround entities within your model. There is a default bounding box for any new model that will surround all entities, including all groups and components. Additionally, there are bounding boxes for Drawingelement objects, including components and groups. Bounding boxes are only large enough to exactly bound the entities within your model, group, or component. You can also create arbitrary BoundingBox objects by calling BoundingBox.new.

constructors.name: initialize
constructors.summary: The new method is used to create a new, empty, bounding box.
constructors.remarks: The new method is used to create a new, empty, bounding box.

methods.name: add
methods.summary: The add method is used to add a point, vertex, or other bounding boxes to the bounding box.
methods.remarks: The add method is used to add a point, vertex, or other bounding boxes to the bounding box. The size of the bounding box will increase as necessary to accommodate the new items. Adding one point to an empty bounding box does not increase the size of the bounding box. You must add at least two points before methods such as BoundingBox.diagonal will return a size greater than zero.

methods.name: center
methods.summary: The center method is used to retrieve the Point3d object at the center of the bounding box.
methods.remarks: The center method is used to retrieve the Point3d object at the center of the bounding box.

returns.doc: the Point3d at the center of the bounding box if successful

methods.name: clear
methods.summary: The clear method is used to clear a bounding box.
methods.remarks: The clear method is used to clear a bounding box. A cleared BoundingBox does not have a size greater than zero until you add at least two points or another bounding box.

returns.doc: the BoundingBox object which was cleared

methods.name: contains?
methods.summary: This method is used to determine if a bounding box contains a specific Point3d or BoundingBox object.
methods.remarks: This method is used to determine if a bounding box contains a specific Point3d or BoundingBox object.

methods.name: corner
methods.summary: The corner method is used to retrieve a point object at a specified corner of the bounding box.
methods.remarks: The corner method is used to retrieve a point object at a specified corner of the bounding box. There are 8 corners to a bounding box, identified by the numbers 0 through 7. Points are returned in the currently set units (inches, by default). These are which index refers to which corner:

params.name: corner_index
params.doc: A number (from 0 to 7) representing point at the corner you want to retrieve.

methods.name: depth
methods.summary: Note: In SketchUp's coordinate system, this corresponds to the height.
methods.remarks: Note: In SketchUp's coordinate system, this corresponds to the height. The #depth method is used to retrieve the Z extents of the bounding box.

methods.name: diagonal
methods.summary: The #diagonal method is used to get the length of the diagonal of the bounding box.
methods.remarks: The #diagonal method is used to get the length of the diagonal of the bounding box.

returns.doc: the size of the diagonal for the bounding box

methods.name: empty?
methods.summary: The empty? method is used to determine if a bounding box is empty (such as if the bounds have not been set.
methods.remarks: The empty? method is used to determine if a bounding box is empty (such as if the bounds have not been set.) This returns the opposite of the valid? method.

methods.name: height
methods.summary: Note: In SketchUp's coordinate system, this corersponds to the depth.
methods.remarks: Note: In SketchUp's coordinate system, this corersponds to the depth. The #height method is used to retrieve the Y extent of the bounding box.

methods.name: intersect
methods.summary: Note: Prior to SU2015 this method would return incorrect result in some cases.
methods.remarks: Note: Prior to SU2015 this method would return incorrect result in some cases. For correct result in these versions you must first check if the boundingboxes actually overlap - then call this to get the resulting boundingbox. The intersect method is used to retrieve a bounding box that is the result of intersecting one bounding box with another.

params.name: boundingbox
params.doc: A second boundbox which might intersect boundingbox1.

returns.doc: the resulting BoundingBox object if successful, an empty BoundingBox object if unsuccessful.

methods.name: max
methods.summary: The max method is used to retrieve the Point3d object where x, y and z are maximum in the bounding box.
methods.remarks: The max method is used to retrieve the Point3d object where x, y and z are maximum in the bounding box. If you attempt to call the max method on an empty bounding box, you will receive a very large negative number.

returns.doc: a Point3d object representing the point where x, y, and z are the maximum in the bounding box.

methods.name: min
methods.summary: The min method is used to retrieve the Point3d where x, y and z are minimum in the bounding box.
methods.remarks: The min method is used to retrieve the Point3d where x, y and z are minimum in the bounding box.

returns.doc: a Point3d object representing the point where x, y, and z are the maximum in the bounding box.

methods.name: valid?
methods.summary: The valid method is used to determine if a bounding box is valid (contains points).
methods.remarks: The valid method is used to determine if a bounding box is valid (contains points).

methods.name: width
methods.summary: The #width method is used to retrieve the X extent of the bounding box.
methods.remarks: The #width method is used to retrieve the X extent of the bounding box.
